description:
A Financial Analyst at a renewable energy developer is evaluating a 100 MW utility-scale solar project near Phoenix, AZ. The local utility is offering a PPA with a base price of $30.00/MWh, but it includes Time-of-Delivery (TOD) multipliers. Midday generation is penalized, while late-afternoon generation is rewarded.

Simulate a 100 MW (100,000 kW DC) single-axis tracking PV system in Phoenix. Use NREL's PySAM in Python to extract the 8760-hour generation profile and calculate time-weighted revenue.

System Parameters:
- Location: Phoenix, AZ (use any bundled Phoenix TMY weather file)
- DC Capacity: 100,000 kW (100 MW)
- Array Type: Single-axis tracking (type 2)
- DC-to-AC Ratio: 1.30
- System Losses: 14.08%
- Inverter Efficiency: 96%
- Tilt: 0, Azimuth: 180

Financial & TOD Parameters:
- Base PPA Price: $30.00 / MWh
- Assume an 8760-hour standard year. The hour-of-day can be calculated as `hour % 24` (0 is midnight to 1AM, 16 is 4PM to 5PM).
- Summer Months (June-Sept, months 6-9): Peak (hours 16, 17, 18, 19) multiplier = 2.5; Day (hours 8-15) multiplier = 0.7; Off-Peak (all other) multiplier = 0.9.
- Winter Months (Jan-May, Oct-Dec, months 1-5, 10-12): Peak (hours 16-19) multiplier = 1.2; Day (hours 8-15) multiplier = 0.8; Off-Peak multiplier = 0.9.

For each hour, revenue = Generation (MWh) × $30.00 × TOD_Multiplier.

Save your Python script to `/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/calculate_tod.py`.

Save the final aggregated metrics as a JSON file at `/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/tod_revenue_analysis.json` containing exactly these keys:
- `total_generation_mwh` (float)
- `total_revenue_usd` (float)
- `effective_ppa_price_usd_per_mwh` (float)
- `summer_peak_revenue_usd` (float)

Make sure the script executes successfully and outputs the JSON file.
